headline: National Language Policy Debate Ignores 23% English Proficiency Gap
context: The 2025 English Language Teaching Centre assessment found a 23-percentage-point gap in English proficiency between urban and rural students, cutting across ethnic lines.

cards.big: The language debate in Malaysia is framed as Malay vs English.
cards.sub: The real gap is urban vs rural. A 23-point proficiency gap separates city and kampung students regardless of ethnicity.

cards.big: Rural Malay students' English proficiency declined 8% between 2018 and 2024.
cards.sub: Urban Malay students improved 3% in the same period. The language divide is economic, not ethnic.

cards.big: PPSMI (teaching science/math in English) was reversed in 2012.
cards.sub: The reversal disproportionately affected rural students who lost their primary English exposure pathway.

cards.big: Graduate unemployment among BM-medium graduates is 28%; English-medium is 11%.
cards.sub: Language proficiency is the strongest predictor of graduate employability after field of study.

cards.big: The language debate is framed as identity. The language data says it is about class.
Strengthening Bahasa Malaysia and English are not contradictory goals. The false binary obscures the real issue: rural students need better English access, not less Malay.
